title: B12 deficiency review

examination.text: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact recognition section supports interval neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery, and functional context without assuming symptoms are caused by B12 deficiency.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 and Boxes 1–2 — recognition, symptoms and risk factors

investigations.text: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.
investigations.rationale: The exact testing section supports clinician review of test type, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, supplements, reference range, and indeterminate results without autonomously interpreting the result.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.16 — tests, result context and interpretation factors

medication.text: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.
medication.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context, and clinician-agreed reassessment without mapping replacement route, dose, or frequency.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.

source_limitations: Exact result, reference range, symptoms, cause, pregnancy, medicine, replacement route or dose, adherence, response, diagnosis, further testing, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
